* the getter is invoked; when it returns a non-null string, an
* `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header is attached to the request.
* Useful for Expo bundles making token-gated API calls.
* NOTE: This function should never be used in web applications where session
* token cookies are automatically associated with API calls by the browser.

# SECURITY: Minimum release age for npm packages (supply-chain attack defense)
# This setting requires that any npm package version must have been published
# for at least 1 day (1440 minutes) before pnpm will allow installing it.
# This is a critical defense against supply-chain attacks. In most cases,
# malicious npm releases are discovered and pulled within hours, so a 1-day
# delay provides a strong safety buffer.
# DO NOT DISABLE THIS SETTING. Removing or setting it to 0 is considered
# extremely dangerous and leaves the entire workspace vulnerable to supply-
# chain attacks, which have been the #1 vector for npm ecosystem compromises.
# If you absolutely need to install a package before the 1-day window has
# passed (e.g. an urgent security bugfix), you can add it to the
# `minimumReleaseAgeExclude` allowlist below. Only consider doing this for
# packages released by trusted organizations with an impeccable security
# posture (e.g. Replit packsges, react from Meta, typescript from Microsoft). Even then,
# remove the exclusion once the 1-day window has passed.
# Exclude @replit scoped packages from the minimum release age check.
# These are published by Replit and trusted — the supply-chain attack vector
# this setting guards against does not apply to our own packages.
# Must be this exact version because expo requires it
# Must be this exact version because expo requires it
# replit uses linux-x64 only, we can exclude all other platforms
# drizzle-kit uses esbuild internally on an older version that's vulnerable, this overrides it
A premium interactive design customization platform where creative professionals discover, customize, and commission bespoke design templates.
- **Framer Motion** — Page transitions, staggered animations, micro-interactions
- **Zustand** — Theme store (dark/light, persisted to localStorage), Customization store
- **React Query** — API data fetching via generated hooks
- Dark mode default, toggled via Zustand `themeStore`
- Custom CSS variables for violet/indigo primary palette
- `/` — Hero landing with animated gradient background, features section
- `/browse` — Template gallery with category filter pills (12 templates, 8 categories)
- `/customize/:id` — Live customization studio with preview panel + tabbed controls
- **In-memory template data** — 12 templates across 8 categories (no database needed)
- **Resend email integration** — Sends notifications when `RESEND_API_KEY` + `ADMIN_EMAIL` are set
- `RESEND_API_KEY` — Optional: enables email notifications on submissions
- `ADMIN_EMAIL` — Optional: admin email for order notifications
- Falls back to CSS gradient when WebGL is unavailable (e.g., in Replit preview)
Premium interactive design customization platform enabling users to explore, customize, and request professionally-crafted design templates through an immersive, glassmorphic interface.
- **Glassmorphism**: Main UI containers with backdrop blur, semi-transparency
- **Lightmorphism**: Subtle background glows and depth layers
- **Motion**: Framer Motion for micro-interactions and page transitions
- **3D Integration**: Limited to hero and template preview—not every element
│  (React Context + Zustand for theme, customization state)  │
| Page | Purpose | Key Features | Data Source |
| `/` (Landing) | Hero showcase | 3D rotating model, value prop, CTA | Static/Config |
| `/browse` | Template gallery | Grid, filtering, preview thumbnail | API: /templates |
| `/customize/[id]` | Main interaction hub | 3D preview, customization panel, form | API: /templates/:id |
| `/confirmation` | Success state | Order summary, next steps, email confirm | URL params/Context |
Persistent across all pages, relatively small, doesn't require re-renders on every change.
**Rationale**: Small, single-source-of-truth for toggling; customization is session-scoped.
Component-level state for UI control and short-lived interactions.
**Rationale**: Separates UI state (Zustand) from server state (SWR).
- `3DPreviewScene`: React Three Fiber preview with interaction
- ✅ Landing hero (hero carousel of rotating templates)
**Interaction Model**: AUTO-ROTATE only, no user interaction
**Performance**: Preload model, use LOD (Level of Detail) if available
│   └── Effects (bloom if dark, subtle glow if light)
- Mouse drag to rotate (X, Y axes only)
Customization panel responsive to changes (subtle feedback)
- Bake colors/materials into model if possible
- Each template has its own 3D model
├── Hero scene: preload on page load
├── Preview scene: lazy load on route change
└── Browser cache: leverage Next.js static optimization
├── Text change → Model updates if possible*
* Limited: Only if model supports text texture swapping
** Always: Materials stored in Three.js Material objects
Purpose: Fetch all templates for browse page
Purpose: Fetch single template details for customize page
Purpose: Submit customized design request (trigger email)
├── Admin email: Full request details + JSON preview
├── Resend failure → 500 Internal Server Error (after retries)
Subject: Your Design Request is Confirmed!
- Next steps: "We'll email you within 24 hours"
setCustomization: (field: string, key: string, value: string) => void;
Next.js App Router: Automatic per-page splitting
├── React Three Fiber: loaded only on pages using 3D
├── Color picker library: loaded on demand
├── Model preview textures: AVIF, optimized atlasing
├── Customize page: preload next template model
├── Canvas resolution: clamp to screen density (not 4K)
├── Model LOD: Use simplified geometry if available
├── Effects: Bloom disabled on low-end devices
├── Model preload: Start on route change, not mount
├── Suspend fallback: Show skeleton while loading
├── Cache: Use browser cache headers + service worker
└── HTTP/2 Server Push: Templates list on landing
├── Rate limiting: 5 requests per IP per hour
├── On failure: retry up to 3 times
├── Alert admin if all retries fail
├── Fallback to in-memory queue during outages
├── Pages: unique title + description per route
├── Headings: h1 per page, proper hierarchy
└── Live regions: aria-live for loading states
├── Dark theme: recalculate contrast per theme
├── Text inputs: trim, max length enforcement
├── Next.js handles CSRF via SameSite cookies
└── No sensitive GET requests (all via POST + validation)
├── Rate limiting: 5 requests per IP per hour
├── Zod/Yup schema validation on all inputs
├── Template ID: verify exists in database
├── No secrets in client code (use middleware)
├── Log all API errors (not sensitive data)
├── Alert on: rate limit breaches, email failures
├── 3D: Manual inspection (Three.js is hard to test)
└── Graceful degradation: no 3D → fallback to images
├── Build: next build (static export where possible)
├── Custom logging: API route errors to file/DB
├── Web Vitals: CLS, FID, LCP (via Vercel Analytics)
├── User funnels: landing → browse → customize → submit
├── CDN: Vercel Edge Network for static content
├── Browser cache: Service Worker for models
├── Async email queue: Bull or Inngest
├── S3 or Cloudflare R2 for model distribution
| **Zustand for theme** | Lightweight, single source of truth, localStorage-friendly |
| **SWR for templates** | Built-in caching, revalidation, simpler than React Query |
| **Next.js API Routes** | Serverless, type-safe with TypeScript, zero additional setup |
| **Resend for email** | Simple API, good deliverability, React component support |
| **3D limited to hero + preview** | Maximize performance; static thumbnails for browse |
| **React Three Fiber only** | Abstraction over Three.js, React-friendly, easier state sync |
| **Glassmorphism as main theme** | Modern, trendy, pairs well with dark mode |
| **Watermorphism for buttons** | Smooth, fluid feel; differentiates from standard buttons |
| **No database initially** | MVP simplicity; scalability added in Phase 2 |
| **Vercel deployment** | Native Next.js support, edge functions, analytics |
✅ Full system architecture (frontend, backend, 3D, state, styling)
This architecture document is a **comprehensive blueprint**. Code should follow this structure exactly:
- API routes: As defined in section 8
## DIAGRAM 4: Data Flow - From Selection to Submission
Fetch from SWR          Store templateId in     Load 3D model
USER EDITS TEXT / CHANGES COLOR / PICKS FONT
API Error ──────► Handler catches ──────► Return error response
│   └── Local state in all feature components
│  ├─ Load R3F only on pages using 3D
│  ├─ LOD (Level of Detail): simplified geo if available
│  ├─ Measure: from route change to first render
# DesignForge Studio - Implementation Checklist & Quick Reference
- [ ] Create Next.js 14 project with App Router
- [ ] Setup Tailwind CSS with custom config (glass, water, light effects)
- [ ] Install dependencies (see dependency list in main architecture)
- [ ] Create initial folder structure (as per file structure section)
- [ ] Add CSS variables file (themes.css: light + dark modes)
- [ ] Add theme toggle to Navbar component
- [ ] Test theme switching on all pages
- [ ] Test all effects on sample components
- [ ] Button component (variants: primary, secondary, glass, outline)
- [ ] Return mock template data (hardcoded or JSON)
- [ ] Test: SWR loading state, error state, data display
- [ ] Create page structure (left panel + right panel layout)
- [ ] Dynamic text inputs based on customizableFields
- [ ] Create side info card (specs, delivery time, pricing)
- [ ] Extract requestId from URL query params
- [ ] Add error boundary (fallback to static image)
- [ ] Optimize: clamp canvas resolution, cull faces
- [ ] Mobile: disable bloom on low-end devices (optional)
- [ ] Material updates based on customization store colors*
- [ ] Mouse drag to rotate (X, Y axes)
- [ ] Text changes → (limited, for now just visual feedback)
- [ ] Performance: monitor render time, optimize LOD if needed
*Note: Material updates only work if models support them. Prioritize rotation/zoom interaction over material updates initially.
- [ ] Pass Zustand color state to 3D materials
- [ ] Update material colors on store change
- [ ] Test real-time preview updates on color change
- [ ] Add loading skeleton while model loads
- [ ] Test store persistence (non-persisted, cleared on navigate)
- [ ] Test store actions and state updates
- [ ] Test: loading states, error states, data display
- [ ] Test end-to-end: select → customize → see updates
- [ ] Handle success: show toast + redirect to /confirmation
- [ ] Handle error: show error toast + suggest retry
- [ ] Request ID, customer info, customizations, deadline
- [ ] Request ID, template preview, next steps
- [ ] Implement retry logic (3 attempts, exponential backoff)
- [ ] Test: emails sent successfully (use Resend sandbox)
- [ ] Test: redirect after submit, display correct data
- [ ] Dynamic imports for React Three Fiber
- [ ] Test: lazy loading on browse page
- [ ] Preload next model on template switch
- [ ] Setup Service Worker for model caching (optional, Phase 2)
- [ ] Create custom metrics (3D load time, API response time)
- [ ] Do NOT log: user emails, API keys, customizations
- [ ] Test all pages (responsive on mobile, tablet, desktop)
- [ ] Test 3D scenes (hero scene rotates, preview scene interactive)
- [ ] Test customize flow (select template → edit → submit → confirm)
- [ ] Test form validation (errors show, submit disabled)
- [ ] Test on multiple browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge)
- [ ] Test on mobile browsers (iOS Safari, Chrome Android)
- [ ] Test slow network (3G simulation, measure load time)
- [ ] Test with keyboard (tab navigation, enter to submit)
- [ ] Test with screen reader (NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver)
- [ ] Measure Core Web Vitals (LCP, FID, CLS)
- [ ] Test with screen reader (all content readable)
- [ ] Test production build locally (next build + next start)
- [ ] Configure environment variables (Resend API key, admin email)
- [ ] Monitor API usage (request count, response time)
- [ ] Check logs daily for first week
- [ ] JSDoc comments on all exported functions
- [ ] README with setup, development, deployment instructions
- [ ] FAQ page (how to customize, submit request)

A premium interactive design customization platform where creative professionals discover, customize, and commission bespoke design templates.
- **Framer Motion** — Page transitions, staggered animations, micro-interactions
- **Zustand** — Theme store (dark/light, persisted to localStorage), Customization store
- **React Query** — API data fetching via generated hooks
- Dark mode default, toggled via Zustand `themeStore`
- Custom CSS variables for violet/indigo primary palette
- `/` — Hero landing with animated gradient background, features section
- `/browse` — Template gallery with category filter pills (12 templates, 8 categories)
- `/customize/:id` — Live customization studio with preview panel + tabbed controls
- **In-memory template data** — 12 templates across 8 categories (no database needed)
- **Resend email integration** — Sends notifications when `RESEND_API_KEY` + `ADMIN_EMAIL` are set
- `RESEND_API_KEY` — Optional: enables email notifications on submissions
- `ADMIN_EMAIL` — Optional: admin email for order notifications
- Falls back to CSS gradient when WebGL is unavailable (e.g., in Replit preview)
lineReports.push({ number, text, length: text.length, flags });
